channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran and the U.S. Have an Understanding. Will It Lead to a Deal?
item.description: Europe and the larger world will be watching carefully to see if talks produce a lasting agreement on Iran’s nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump in Évian-les-Bains, France, for the Group of 7 meeting on Wednesday.

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Moscow Refinery in Large-Scale Drone Attack
item.description: The attack, which shut down the capital’s airports for several hours, was part of an escalating campaign to bring the conflict home to Russians.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne and Nataliya Vasilyeva
item.media:credit: via Reuters
item.media:description: The scene over the area of an oil refinery on the outskirts of Moscow on Thursday.

item.title: A Twist in Ukraine’s Drone Campaign Is ‘Really Hurting the Russians’
item.description: Midrange attacks, using upgraded drones that Ukraine produces in huge numbers, are causing fuel shortages and complicating troop rotations.
item.dc:creator: Marc Santora
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Soldiers preparing to launch drones at targets in Russia from an undisclosed location in Ukraine last month.

item.title: Knicks in Five. The City Is Alive.
item.description: New York City found a reason to party on Thursday, cheering, crying and climbing around Broadway to celebrate the Knicks’ N.B.A. championship.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg
item.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times

item.title: See the Knicks Championship Parade Unfold
item.description: Fans gathered in Manhattan on Thursday to cheer on the N.B.A. champions at a parade that has been 53 years in the making.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times and Maya King
item.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times

item.title: Jose Alvarado the Photographer Looks Back on the Knicks’ Magic
item.description: While fans watched their team, José A. Alvarado Jr. watched the crowd.
item.dc:creator: John Otis and José A. Alvarado Jr.
item.media:description: In the West Village neighborhood during Game 5 of the N.B.A. finals.

item.title: While Knicks Fans Celebrate, a New York Artist Fumes
item.description: After the Knicks promoted Gavin Snider’s watercolor painting in the wake of their N.B.A. title, one of the most famous artists on social media posted an uncannily similar work.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Small and Frank Reneau

item.title: Takeaways From ‘Regime Change,’ Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan’s Book on Trump’s White House
item.description: The book by Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan of The New York Times, “Regime Change,” reveals a host of details and surprising exchanges as President Trump pushed to drastically expand his power.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump (Book)
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new book by two New York Times reporters describes President Trump’s efforts to bend the federal government, cultural institutions and news cycles to his will.

item.title: Colorado Governor Overruled His Own Clemency Board to Release Tina Peters
item.description: Two members of Gov. Jared Polis’s board that reviews commutation requests came forward to say the secretive panel had twice rejected the release of Tina Peters, convicted of election tampering.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy
item.media:credit: Chet Strange for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hannah Proff and Azra Taslimi, two Denver lawyers who serve on Colorado’s 11-person clemency advisory board, say the board twice rejected requests to release Tina Peters.

item.title: ICE Spent $700 Million on 7 Warehouses. Now It Wants to Get Rid of Them.
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement plans to sell or give away most of the 11 warehouses it bought to detain migrants, reversing course on a signature initiative.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:credit: Rachel Wisniewski for The New York Times
item.media:description: The warehouse that ICE recently purchased for $87 million in Hamburg, Pa.

item.title: Trump Redirects Millions From Secret Service Amid White House Construction
item.description: The White House insists that private donations are paying for a new ballroom, but the administration is using taxpayer money for a series of security upgrades.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Construction on the White House grounds in May. President Trump has linked his ballroom project to national security and an underground bunker at the White House.

item.title: Trump Administration Backs Off Plan to End Ocean Monitoring System
item.description: The reversal comes after the Senate passed a bipartisan bill on Wednesday to block the removal of deep-sea monitoring instruments.
item.dc:creator: Maxine Joselow
item.media:credit: Darlene Trew Crist/WHOI, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A data-collecting buoy off the Carolina coast. The National Science Foundation was planning to begin removing hundreds of ocean instruments that measure ocean data this month.

item.title: McCarthy Aide’s Tell-All Book Recounts Trump’s Expletive-Filled Threats to G.O.P.
item.description: A new book by John Leganski, the floor manager for former Speaker Kevin McCarthy, offers a rare behind-the-scenes account of how President Trump wields his power through threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.category: Glory, Grief, and the Gavel: An Inside Guide to Running for Speaker of the House (Book)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: John Leganski, in an orange tie, with Representative Kevin McCarthy when he was elected speaker of the House in January 2023.

item.title: Scores Fall Ill at Air Force Base After Hegseth Makes Flu Vaccine Optional
item.description: The defense secretary described the vaccine requirement, which he lifted in April, as an “absurd, overreaching” mandate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Maggie Haberman
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Lackland Air Force Base (San Antonio, Tex)
item.media:credit: Chip Somodevilla/Getty Images
item.media:description: Airmen at Lackland Air Force Base last year. A flu outbreak at the base has raced through a training wing among new recruits.

item.title: Gary Shteyngart, a Russian-Born Author, Tours Thomas Jefferson’s Home With His Son
item.description: I emigrated from the Soviet Union decades ago, and recently toured Thomas Jefferson’s home with my American-born history-buff son.
item.dc:creator: Gary Shteyngart and Demetrius Freeman
item.media:description: The author and his son walk toward Thomas Jefferson’s residence at Monticello.

item.title: Representative Tom Kean to Return to Congress in 12 Days, Aide Says
item.description: Mr. Kean has missed three months of votes in Washington and been absent from the campaign trail in his New Jersey district because of an unexplained health condition.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully
item.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mr. Kean has missed 135 floor votes since March 5.

item.title: Democratic Socialist Defeats Centrist in the D.C. Mayoral Primary
item.description: President Trump’s grip on the capital loomed over the race, and he threatened to tighten it if Councilwoman Janeese Lewis George is elected. She almost certainly will be.
item.dc:creator: Clyde McGrady

item.title: The Major Oak, Ancient Tree of Robin Hood Legend, Has Died
item.description: The Major Oak in the Sherwood Forest was between 800 and 1,200 years old. It succumbed to a combination of over-tourism, climate change and misguided efforts to save it.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:credit: Royal Society for the Protection of Birds
item.media:description: The Major Oak in Sherwood Forest Nature Reserve in Nottinghamshire, England, in May.

item.title: In Ancient Pits Near Stonehenge, Scientists See Hints of Solstice Ritual
item.description: British archaeologists may have found the remains of a site where people celebrated the solstice thousands of years ago, a few miles from the famed stone circle.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg

item.title: Barack Obama Presidential Center Opens in Chicago
item.description: Thursday’s opening drew four former presidents, as well as Bruce Springsteen, Bono and Jennifer Hudson. Chicago has seen years of planning and legal fights.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman
item.category: Obama, Barack H, Presidential Library and Museum
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Obama family onstage at the Obama Presidential Center opening ceremony in Chicago on Thursday.

item.title: Here’s What Barack and Michelle Obama Said at His Presidential Center Opening
item.description: The Obamas each took to the microphone, delivering speeches before a crowd of dignitaries, elected officials and Democratic supporters.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman
item.category: Obama, Barack H, Presidential Library and Museum
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Obama family at the grand opening ceremony for the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago on Thursday.

item.title: Stephen Colbert Nods to Obama’s Tan Suit at Presidential Center Opening
item.description: Celebrity guests paid winking homage to President Obama’s notorious tan suit.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Win Mcnamee/Getty Images
item.media:description: A pair of funny guys in shades of khaki at the Obama presidential library opening on Thursday.

item.title: Mariska Hargitay on ‘Every Brilliant Thing’ and Finding Inspiration From Jalen Brunson
item.description: The “Law & Order: SVU” actress “was really scared” about starring in “Every Brilliant Thing.” Who does she look to for inspiration? The Knicks, of course.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.media:credit: Lila Barth for The New York Times

item.title: JD Vance on the Morality of the Trump Administration
item.description: I asked the vice president what is Christian about this White House.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat and Victoria Chamberlin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith (Book)
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Don’t Blame Plunging Birthrates on Phones
item.description: Low fertility rates are a lagging indicator, the final outcome of a string of social shifts.
item.dc:creator: Christine Emba
item.media:credit: Margaret Murphy for The New York Times

item.title: Supreme Court Narrows Law Banning Drug Users From Owning Guns
item.description: The justices sided with a Texas gun owner who faced criminal charges after admitting to marijuana use argued that a federal gun law violated the Second Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.category: New York State Rifle & Pistol Association v Bruen (Supreme Court Decision)
item.media:credit: Shelby Tauber for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Hemani’s suburban home was raided by federal agents in August 2022 after his family had come under suspicion because of its ties to Iran.
